# Questions in Livestream 164

Live Stream #164: Is Dr. Falk The Biblical David?

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=772" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:52</a> | Is postmortem sanctification possible, and does that fit with sola gratia? | He says it is possible in principle, though the harder question is how likely it is, and he rejects soul sleep. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=894" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:54</a> | Do some people sleep until the Lord's return? | He says no; "sleep" is a euphemism for death, not a doctrine of soul sleep. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=1034" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:14</a> | In Acts 18, can the word usually translated "witness" be read as "martyr" instead? | He says the context supports "witness" rather than the later technical sense of martyr. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=1243" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:43</a> | If a church focuses mostly on the New Testament, how should the Old Testament be taught? | He recommends alternating Old and New Testament teaching so the congregation receives both foundations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=1493" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:53</a> | Were there once 70 million mummies that were ground up for medicine and other products? | He says yes and no: many mummies were used that way, but most were animal mummies rather than human mummies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=1924" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:04</a> | Does 1 Peter 5:5 use a word for putting on a slave's apron? | He treats that sermon claim cautiously and says the passage is about humility and service rather than a neat lexical gimmick. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=2232" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:12</a> | Was Joseph entirely innocent in Genesis 37? | He says Joseph was not completely blameless, but the brothers' response still goes far beyond anything Joseph deserved. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=2733" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:33</a> | How did Sheerah manage to build three cities? | He says the text does not explain how, only that she did, and the point is her resourcefulness after family loss. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=2785" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:25</a> | What should readers make of Finkelstein's claim that many of Joshua's conquered cities did not exist at the time? | He says the claim needs heavy nuancing because conquest discussions depend on which sites and timelines are actually in view. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=2946" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:06</a> | Does Finkelstein use the early Exodus date in good faith? | He says Finkelstein critiques the early date far more than the late date, even though he does not affirm the late date either. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=3191" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">53:11</a> | Is Aten a god or the name for the afterlife? | He says Aten is the solar disc god, while the Egyptian afterlife is the Duat. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=3344" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:44</a> | Why are shellfish, camels, hare, and similar animals prohibited? | He says the ceremonial laws shaped an antithetical worldview around clean and unclean categories. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=3580" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:40</a> | Does pork have worms and parasites? | He says it used to, but modern pork production is much cleaner. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=3709" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:49</a> | How should Horemheb's death and crown-prince chronology be reconciled? | He says the question mixes two different chronological systems. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=3846" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:04:06</a> | If Job is Iron Age wisdom literature, could Solomon have written it? | He says probably not, because Job reflects Aramaic cultural context that does not fit Solomon well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=3956" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:56</a> | Could Job simply be a novel meant to give wisdom? | He says "novel" is an anachronistic category and Job is better treated as wisdom literature. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=4043" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:23</a> | Is Job one of the older books of the Old Testament? | He says that idea is a late scholarly myth and dates Job more plausibly to the Iron Age. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=4325" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:05</a> | If theosis was the expected hope in Egypt after death, what made Pharaoh special as a potential god? | He says pharaohs had the power of life and death and were viewed as godlike in a way that changed over time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=4690" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:10</a> | Is Heli in Luke 3:23 best understood as Mary's father? | He says yes, treating Luke as the maternal line while Matthew gives the legal line through Joseph. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=5403" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:03</a> | If Miriam is an Egyptian name, why did later Jews take up the name? | He says it means "beloved" and has no theophoric element, so it could be adopted into Israelite culture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=5574" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:54</a> | What does Dr. Falk mean when he says Eastern Orthodox critics misunderstand sola Scriptura? | He says they often confuse sola Scriptura with nuda Scriptura. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=5855" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:37:35</a> | How does divorce in the area of abandonment work when there is no certificate of divorce? | He says that situation is messy; modern no-fault divorce removes one problem, but ancient abandonment left women in a harsh position. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=6086" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:26</a> | What kinds of spirit creatures are there, and how do they relate to angels, demons, and divine council beings? | He says the categories are diverse and include more than just angels, demons, unclean spirits, and council members. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=7552" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:05:52</a> | Who was Darius the Mede in Daniel 5:31, and is he the same Darius as Daniel 9:1? | He works through the Daniel passages and treats the identification as a chronological and textual question. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=8264" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:44</a> | Does Eve being created within the garden suggest a special relationship to the inner world of the garden? | He says possibly, especially if Eden is understood as a temple setting, but he keeps the conclusion tentative. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=8387" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:47</a> | What does Dr. Falk think about reported evidence of an Assyrian camp that supports the Bible? | He says it sounds provocative if true, but he needs to read more before evaluating it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=8978" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:38</a> | Where do evil spirits or demons originate in the Bible? | He says they are part of the ancient Near Eastern worldview, and the Bible assumes that broader context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=9047" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:47</a> | Is there a lore reason for demons, and did God create them? | He says spirit beings are part of creation, but the biblical texts are high-context and do not spell everything out. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=9734" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:14</a> | How should someone discuss Preston Sprinkle's arguments with an affirming Christian friend? | He advises care and attention to how the discussion is handled. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=10265" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:51:05</a> | Are the daughters of Adam a contrast to the sons of God? | He says yes in a way, contrasting priestly identity with ancestor-oriented worship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=10853" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:00:53</a> | What is the relationship between the image at the top of the Re-Harakhti stela and a similar winged figure? | He says he does not know the other figure, but identifies Re-Harakhti as the solar disc with the solar serpent. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=11017" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:37</a> | Is it true that cultic prostitution did not exist in the world during Leviticus? | He says no; cultic prostitution existed long before Leviticus, including in Mesopotamia. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=11216" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:06:56</a> | Might the Indo-Aryan Mitanni elite have had the Vedas? | He says it is possible, but the Mitanni left no writing that would let us know. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=11402" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:10:02</a> | Were Ham or his sons deified as gods under other names in ancient Egypt? | He says not to current knowledge; Egyptian records do not mention them that way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=11474" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:11:14</a> | What does Dr. Falk think of Patrick D. Miller's work on Israelite religion? | He says he does not know Miller's work and usually turns to Richard Hess on early Israelite religion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=11601" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:13:21</a> | In Egypt and Turkey, is it permitted to talk openly about Jesus Christ in public? | He says not in Turkey, while Egypt is more nuanced and depends on setting and initiation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=12536" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:28:56</a> | What does epiousion mean in Matthew 6:11? | He says the word is controversial and walks through possible meanings in the Lord's Prayer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=13282" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:41:22</a> | Is there a correlation between human sacrifice associated with bulls in Minoan, Canaanite, and Carthaginian contexts? | He says Carthage and Canaan are connected through Phoenician origins, but Minoan connection is not really established. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=13643" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:47:23</a> | Did the cult of Yahweh come from Canaan or the Kenites? | He says the Kenite hypothesis seems more likely to him than a northern Levant origin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=14016" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:53:36</a> | How old are the roots of Genesis 1:3? | He says the oral roots are hard to date and could be thousands of years old. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l7sVUZAXkas?t=14520" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:02:00</a> | Since the Ark of the Covenant is called God's footstool, could the cherubim have formed an actual throne? | He says an empty miniature throne is possible, but the text does not tell us. |
